conversation_quality.criteria: Evaluate the accuracy and relevance of duplicate detection analysis, including correct identification of duplicate and near-duplicate records, appropriate use of similarity thresholds, and clear explanation of deduplication strategies.
evaluation_steps: Verify the agent correctly identifies duplicate and near-duplicate rows using appropriate matching logic.
Check that similarity thresholds and matching criteria are clearly explained and justified.
Assess whether deduplication recommendations are actionable and include impact estimates.
Confirm the agent explains trade-offs between exact and fuzzy matching approaches.

task_completion.criteria: Evaluate whether the agent successfully identifies duplicates, quantifies their impact on storage and performance, and produces actionable DELETE or MERGE SQL to remediate duplicates.
evaluation_steps: Check that duplicate detection queries were executed against the target tables.
Verify the agent produced a summary of duplicate counts, affected rows, and storage impact.
Confirm actionable SQL (DELETE, MERGE, or dedup INSERT/SELECT) was generated for remediation.
Assess whether the agent validated results by comparing before and after row counts.

trajectory_efficiency.criteria: Evaluate whether the agent efficiently identifies duplicates without redundant table scans or unnecessary queries against DBC views.
evaluation_steps: Check that the agent sampled data before running full-table duplicate scans.
Verify the agent did not run redundant or overlapping queries for the same duplicate check.
Assess whether the agent used appropriate GROUP BY and HAVING clauses rather than self-joins where possible.
Confirm the agent minimized the number of queries needed to reach a conclusion.
